conversations.title: M.I.A Agent answers the real questions.
conversations.subtitle: Not a generic script — an assistant that knows the context of every reading, and that declines the questions it must not answer.
conversations.footnote: The answers above are scripted for the demo. In production, M.I.A Agent uses Claude (Anthropic) with the InsightSignal v2.1.0 context injected and a system prompt that forbids any personalised recommendation.

honesty.title: An honest indicator starts by saying what it does not do.
honesty.subtitle: MIA describes the market — the structure, the regime, the events. It promises no gains and does not present itself as a trading system. We own this publicly, because it is the only defensible stance for a tool for understanding.
honesty.quote: No market indicator should promise gains. We make none. What we offer is an augmented understanding of the market — not a financial performance.
honesty.never3: Sell a reading as a trade signal.
honesty.never4: Present ourselves as a profitable trading system.
honesty.today1: A structured reading of the market in real time.
honesty.today3: A chatbot that declines trade orders.
honesty.today4: Transparent technical documentation of our algorithms.
honesty.cta: How our indicator describes the market

pricing.title: One plan. The whole tool, no half-measures.
pricing.intro: No disguised trial, no tier that cripples the tool: full access, cancelable in one click. The demos on this page are free — you see exactly what you get. See our stance in the <link>Honesty</link> section.
pricing.planSubtitle: A single subscription, the complete tool — reading, chatbot, scanner, zones.
pricing.annualBilling: Billed {total} $ per year — a saving of 120 $ (−20 %).
pricing.feature1: Unlimited XAU/USD + EUR/USD readings · M15 → H4
pricing.feature2: All sections: SMC structure, regime, volatility, events
pricing.feature3: Unlimited M.I.A Agent chatbot (Claude, reading context injected)
pricing.feature4: Multi-combo scanner + Zones page (OB / FVG lifecycle)
pricing.feature6: Detailed expert synthesis + academic RAG sources
pricing.reassurance1: No card trap. Cancel in one click from your account, with no follow-up calls or retention questionnaire.
pricing.reassurance2: No fixed-term commitment. The monthly plan can be canceled anytime; the annual plan stays active until the end of the paid period.
pricing.reassurance3: Your data stays yours. Readings and conversations are private, never resold, never cross-referenced for advertising.
pricing.reassurance4: Try before you pay. The demos on this page are interactive and free — you see exactly what you're buying.
pricing.b2bDescription: Brokers, funds, media: REST API integration + signed webhooks, SLA and guided onboarding — pricing on request.
pricing.disclaimer: Early-access demonstration. MIA Markets produces contextual editorial analyses, not personalized recommendations.

faq.title: You're probably wondering…
faq.q1: Is MIA a trading signal service?
faq.a1: No. MIA Markets produces descriptive market readings (structure, regime, events, narrated reading) on an asset and a timeframe. The chatbot explicitly refuses “should I buy?” type questions. No personalized recommendation is delivered.
faq.q2: Does MIA promise performance or gains?
faq.a2: No. MIA is an augmented-understanding tool, not a performance engine: it describes the market (structure, regime, events) without promising returns or presenting itself as a trading system. What we measure and stand by publicly is the descriptive fidelity of our readings — detailed in the Honesty section.
faq.q3: How does the M.I.A Agent chatbot work?
faq.a3: M.I.A Agent is MIA Markets' conversational assistant. It uses Claude (Anthropic) with the current reading's context injected into the system: structure, regime, upcoming macro events, instrument and timeframe. The system prompt forbids any personalized recommendation. If the API key is missing, an educational scripted fallback takes over — no hallucinated answer will be served.
faq.q4: Am I eligible for MIA Markets?
faq.a4: You must be at least 18, accept the terms of use and confirm that you understand the non-personalized nature of the analysis. The restrictions that apply in your jurisdiction are detailed in the terms.
faq.a5: One-click cancellation from your account area — no sales follow-up, no retention questionnaire. The subscription stays active until the end of the period already paid for.
faq.q6: What happens to my history and my data?
faq.a6: Your readings and your chatbot conversations stay private. You can request access to, correction of, or erasure of your data at any time (contact@mia.markets). No resale, no advertising cross-referencing. Details in the privacy policy.
faq.methodologyPrompt: Want to dig into how it works?
faq.methodologyLink: How our indicator describes market structures

beforeAfter.subtitle: Most traders stack three indicators that contradict each other. MIA reads the market's structure and shows it as-is — here's the product's real chart, on the right.

chart.title: Three contradictory indicators
chart.desc: An illustrative chart where RSI, MACD and Bollinger bands send opposing signals over the same time window.

howItWorks.title: From the raw candle to a reading you understand.
howItWorks.subtitle: MIA reads the market like a structural analyst, then explains it to you in plain language. Here are the four steps — and, further down, what each demo on this page shows you under real conditions.

marketStructure.title: 1 · Market structure
marketStructure.body: The engine spots the real footprints of big flows: breaks of structure (BOS), changes of character (CHOCH), Order Blocks, Fair Value Gaps and retests. No stacked indicators, no subjective interpretation.

regimeVolatility.title: 2 · Regime & volatility
regimeVolatility.body: It qualifies the trend, the maturity of the move and the average candle range — normal or elevated volatility. What is uncertain is shown as uncertain, never hidden.

macroEvents.title: 3 · Macro events
macroEvents.body: It spots upcoming high-impact releases (FOMC, ECB, NFP…) and flags the likely widening of volatility itself — you know what to expect before the announcement, it's up to you to judge.

narratedChatbot.title: 4 · Narrated reading + chatbot
narratedChatbot.body: Everything is translated into a clear reading, then M.I.A Agent answers your questions with that context injected. It explains, it contextualizes — and it refuses any buy or sell order.

multiAsset.title: Multi-asset, multi-horizon
multiAsset.body: The same methodological rigor on gold and the euro, from M15 to H4 — BTC comes after the engine is validated.

contextChatbot.title: A chatbot with the context
contextChatbot.body: M.I.A Agent knows the current reading: structure, regime, events. It explains in plain terms, and refuses the questions it shouldn't answer.

singleReading.title: A single reading, owned
singleReading.body: No more three indicators contradicting each other: a single framework, a descriptive verdict, uncertainty always visible.

honestByDesign.title: Honest by design
honestByDesign.body: Zero promise of gains, zero trade signal — an educational stance we stand by publicly.

cookies.title: Cookies & privacy
cookies.description: We use cookies to run the site (necessary) and, subject to your consent, to remember your preferences, measure audience and improve the product. You can accept, reject or customise at any time.

cookies.functionalDesc: Remember your preferences (language, expanded sections, chatbot state).
cookies.analyticsDesc: Anonymised usage statistics to improve the product (V2: Plausible self-hosted, with no transfer outside the EU).
cookies.marketingDesc: Personalisation of any communications. No tool active in V1.

samples._comment: Landing demo MarketReading prose (conditions.description, event potential_effect_description) is intentionally NOT keyed here yet: it lives on the MarketReading data contract in lib/market-reading/fixtures.ts and renders through the SHARED MarketReadingCard sections (ConditionsSection/EventsSection) that are also consumed by the off-limits app/zones/scanner surfaces. Localizing it cleanly requires a data-contract change touching forbidden files, so it is deferred. See report.

chatbot._comment: French source-of-truth mirror of mocks/chatbot_responses.json + the code FALLBACK_SCRIPT. Consumers resolve these via useChatbotScriptGetter/useChatbotScriptOrFallback in lib/chatbot.ts, keyed by scriptKey (SCRIPT_KEY_BY_ID) then question id. Numeric/enum reading fields stay localized downstream by useReadingFormatters. The buy-or-not / donc-je-dois-vendre compliance refusals are LEGALLY LOAD-BEARING — keep verbatim.

why-this-score.text: What does this reading tell me?
why-this-score.reply: This reading describes a rather bullish market. The price broke above a recent high (a break of structure), then came back to test the broken level before bouncing: this is what we call a retest. There is also an imbalance zone left by a fast move (a Fair Value Gap) between 2378 and 2381, still unfilled, and an active Order Block just below. The overall context is a bullish trend, with volatility (the average range of the candles) remaining normal. None of this is an instruction: it is a snapshot of the current structure, to do with as you see fit.

armed-retest.reply: When the price breaks a high or a low, it often comes back to touch the broken level before moving on: this “visit” is called a retest. The reading tracks the state of this retest: either no break has occurred, or the break is done and we are waiting for the price to return, or the price has come back to touch the level and held, or it has crossed back through in the other direction and the zone no longer holds. It is a structural marker that is often observed, but often observed does not mean certain: it is a description of the market, not an entry instruction.

fomc-impact.reply: The FOMC minutes are released in about 18 hours. It is a high-impact event for gold: historically, gold moves much more than usual in the window surrounding the release (from 30 min before to 1 h after). Concretely, two things: (1) any position open now will go through the event, (2) MIA flags the upcoming release and warns you in advance of the likely widening of volatility around it — for you to take into account. To anticipate the direction of the move you would need to cross-reference market expectations — which the engine does not do yet.

historical-comparison.text: What does it look like historically?
historical-comparison.reply: I'd rather be honest: our reliability tests on past data are not finished yet. Rather than quote you unvalidated performance figures, I give you no returns, success rate or statistics until the validation is complete. What I can tell you today: there are 7 years of clean historical data and a transparent description method, detailed on our Methodology page. Transparency before the promise — that is precisely what sets MIA Markets apart.

buy-or-not.reply: No, I give no instruction to buy, sell or time the market. My role is to describe the market structure and help you understand it — not to decide for you. What I can describe: there is a confirmed bullish break, a retest holding around 2378-2381, an invalidation of this structure if the price drops clearly back below 2378, and a macro event in 18 hours that can move everything. What only you can decide: whether your risk management, your horizon and your allocation justify a position. MIA Markets is a tool for understanding, not a signals service.

why-this-score.text: What does this reading tell me?
why-this-score.reply: This reading describes a market that is hard to read for now. There was indeed a bearish break, but the price has not yet come back to test it: as long as that retest has not happened, the structure remains fragile. The imbalance zone (Fair Value Gap) is modest, and the market is mostly moving within a low-volatility range — no clear trend. Somewhat bearish news weighs slightly in the same direction. In short: few elements aligned, so a cautious reading and a wider-than-usual displayed uncertainty.

armed-retest.text: And where is the retest in this reading?
armed-retest.reply: It is still awaited. The bearish break at 1.08750 occurred 4 candles ago over 1 hour, but the price has not yet climbed back to touch the broken level. The zones to watch are 1.0860-1.0868 (Fair Value Gap) and 1.0870-1.0885 (Order Block). As long as this return has not happened, the reading remains fragile: many breaks without a retest turn out to be false breaks. It is a description of the structure, not an invitation to take a position.

event-impact.text: The ECB in 3 hours, what does that change?
event-impact.reply: A lot. The ECB decision is a very high-impact event for EUR/USD: volatility (the range of the candles) typically explodes in the window surrounding the announcement. MIA flags the upcoming release and warns you in advance of this likely widening of volatility. Any position open now will go entirely through the decision — this is exactly the kind of context where the current structure, already fragile (retest not yet done), becomes even more uncertain.

historical-comparison.reply: Our reliability tests on past data are still in progress. So I will quote you no return, success rate or statistic until the validation is complete. What I can describe structurally: this reading combines several weak elements (retest not done, market in a range, imminent ECB decision). It is typically a configuration where the reading remains cautious. Transparency before the promise.

buy-or-not.reply: No, and I will be even clearer than usual: in this reading, many elements are uncertain (retest not done, market in a range, wide uncertainty, ECB decision in 3 hours). There are many technical reasons to stay cautious. But it is not for me to make that decision — it is for you to weigh your risk management, your tolerance for event-driven moves, your reading of the fundamental context and your current position. My role stops at describing what I see.

why-this-score.text: Why is this reading so quiet?
why-this-score.reply: Because most of the elements are silent. No recent break of structure, no significant Order Block, and a single Fair Value Gap present but without confirmed direction. The market is in a low-volatility range (the candles move little). The news is nearly neutral. This reading honestly reflects a market in wait-and-see mode: there is not much directional to say. It is also the engine's healthiest reading — when nothing is happening, saying “nothing is happening.” The displayed uncertainty is wide, because the situation is ambiguous.

armed-retest.reply: A retest is when the price, after breaking a level, comes back to touch it before moving on. On the current reading (quiet H4), no break has occurred, so there is nothing to retest for now. The only active technical element is a Fair Value Gap between 2358 and 2362, still untouched. The market must first break something (a high or a low) before we can talk about a retest.

event-impact.text: The NFP in 2 days, what does that change?
event-impact.reply: It is too far off to affect the current H4 reading, but it shapes the next 2 days. Around the NFP release, MIA will warn you of the likely widening of volatility. On H4, that means 1 to 2 candles will be affected. If your horizon is shorter than the time until then, the event invalidates nothing current. If you open an H4 position now, it will live through the NFP — a moment when volatility (the range of the candles) rises sharply.

historical-comparison.text: What does a consolidation like this look like historically?
historical-comparison.reply: Our reliability tests on past data are still in progress. For a quiet configuration like this one, I will quote you no return or statistic until the validation is complete. What I can describe: this reading identifies no strong technical trigger (no break, no significant Order Block, trendless market). In this kind of context, observing and noting the key levels is often more telling than acting. That is also why the reading remains cautious.

buy-or-not.reply: Once again, I give no instruction. What I can describe factually: the current reading identifies no strong technical trigger (no break, no significant Order Block, trendless market). If your approach looks for entries with a clear structural rationale, this reading offers few. But it is for you to weigh that against your personal context. No performance figures will be published until our reliability tests are complete — that is a rule of transparency.

fallback.text: Why don't I see any questions?
fallback.reply: I didn't find a scripted scenario for this specific reading. This is temporary — the real-time response engine integration is coming in the next backend integration sprint.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.pendingNotice: Structured preliminary version. The final text (legally reviewed) will be published with the legal terminal. The structure below faithfully describes the processing currently in effect.

donnees.title: 2. Data processed
donnees.body: Username, email address, password (hashed, never in plain text), consents (version + timestamp), session data. No bank card data is collected by MIA Markets.

bases.title: 3. Legal bases
bases.body: Performance of the contract (account and service), legitimate interest (security), consent (acceptance of the Terms and of this Policy at registration).

droits.title: 4. Your rights (GDPR / Law 25)
droits.body: Access, rectification, erasure, portability, restriction and objection. Any request to loukmanebessam@gmail.com — response within 30 days.

conservation.title: 5. Retention
conservation.body: Account data is retained for the duration of the account. Sessions expire automatically. Retention details by category will be specified in the final version.

contact.title: 6. Contact
contact.body: For any question about your data: loukmanebessam@gmail.com.

meta.title: Methodology — How MIA Markets describes the market
meta.description: Transparent technical documentation: how our indicator detects SMC structures (Order Block, Fair Value Gap, breaks), how we describe the displayed elements, our data source, and what we do not do.

header.title: How MIA Markets describes the market
header.intro: This page explains, without jargon, how our indicator spots market structures and what each displayed element means. It is descriptive documentation: we explain what the engine observes, never what it would predict.

engagement.title: Our commitment
engagement.intro: MIA Markets is a tool for understanding the market, not a trading system. Our stance is descriptive (“augmented reading” level): we describe a structure, we never recommend an action.
engagement.quote: No market indicator should promise gains. We make no such promise. What we offer is an augmented understanding of the market — not financial performance.
engagement.caption: — MIA Markets public commitment, May 27, 2026

concepts.title: Structure concepts (SMC)
concepts.intro: Our reading relies on Smart Money Concepts — a framework for reading price structure. Here are the elements the engine detects and displays.

detection.order-block: We spot the last candle of the opposite direction just before a marked move. It is the price zone from which the move started — often where significant orders accumulated. The engine tracks its state: active, mitigated (already retouched) or invalidated.
detection.fvg: We detect a price “gap” left by three consecutive candles where the first and the third do not overlap: a move too fast for all trades to take place. The engine measures the size of this gap and tracks whether it remains open, partially filled or filled.
detection.bos: We compare the price to recent highs and lows (the “swings”). When it clearly breaks above the last high (or below the last low) in the direction of the trend, it is a break of structure: the current trend is confirmed.
detection.choch: Same logic as a break of structure, but in the OPPOSITE direction to the recent trend. It is the first possible sign of a structural reversal — described as such, without inferring a future direction.
detection.retest: After a break, the engine watches whether the price comes back to touch the broken level. It tracks the state of the return: not yet returned, being tested, confirmed (the level holds) or invalidated (the price crossed it in the opposite direction).
detection.mtf: The same reading is computed on several timeframes (15 min, 1 h, 4 h, 1 day…). We display the bias of each side by side: when several durations point in the same direction, the structure is clearer. None is weighted to produce a directional score.

scores.title: What we describe (and how)
scores.intro: Some elements of the reading are qualified (importance of a zone, status, phase, uncertainty). Here is what each describes and the variables that make it up — none is a probability of success.

formulas.variablesLabel: What the engine takes into account

order-block.title: Order Block
order-block.description: A zone left by an imbalance candle before a marked move. We display it at its formation with its price range and its present state (active, tested, mitigated, invalidated) — with no rating or quality ranking, no probability of success.

variables.0: the price range of the zone
variables.1: its size relative to recent volatility
variables.2: its current state: active, tested, mitigated or invalidated

fvg-force.title: Status of a Fair Value Gap
fvg-force.description: Describes the state of an imbalance zone: active, partially filled or filled. The relative size of the gap indicates its magnitude, without predicting when it will be filled.

variables.0: the size of the gap relative to recent average volatility
variables.1: the number of candles elapsed since its creation
variables.2: the share already filled by the price

phase-de-marche.title: Market phase
phase-de-marche.description: Three descriptive labels side by side — trend, volatility, phase (accumulation / trend / distribution / range / expansion). A snapshot of the observed state, never an instruction.

variables.0: the direction and slope of recent highs/lows
variables.1: the average range of the candles (volatility)

incertitude.title: Observed uncertainty range
incertitude.description: The price gap typically observed recently around the reading. It is a measure of uncertainty displayed honestly — not a forecast range or a target.

variables.0: the recent dispersion of prices, measured by the ATR (Average True Range, the standard average of the candles' range)

donnees.title: Our data source
donnees.detail: Market data via the Twelve Data API (free tier, 800 requests per day in V1).
donnees.coverage: XAU/USD and EUR/USD, on 15 minutes, 1 hour and 4 hours.

limites.title: What we do not do
limites.intro: Transparency also applies to our limits. Deliberately, the indicator does none of the following.

items.0: Predict the direction or magnitude of a move.
items.1: Issue a trade signal, an entry or exit point.
items.2: Calculate a price target, a stop-loss or a take-profit.
items.4: Promise a gain, a return or a performance.

attributions.title: Attributions
attributions.intro: The candlestick chart in the reading workspace relies on an open-source library, credited here as its license requires.
attributions.license: <name>Lightweight Charts™</name> — © <tv>TradingView, Inc.</tv>, distributed under the <link>Apache 2.0</link> license. Chart display library used to render the candles and zones.
attributions.note: Note: this is a display library only. MIA Markets uses no TradingView API or market data feed — the prices shown come from our own engine.

seo.title: MIA Markets — Conversational market indicator
seo.description: MIA Markets (Multi-asset Intelligence Assistant) — XAU/USD and FX market reading, explained by a quant. Sourced contextual analyses, educational stance.
seo.ogDescription: Contextual algorithmic readings for XAU/USD and forex, explained by a chatbot (M.I.A Agent). Educational stance.
seo.twitterDescription: Understand the market — without being told what to do. Algorithmic readings + M.I.A Agent chatbot.

zones.title: Zones
zones.intro: The lifecycle of each detected zone — formation, tests, mitigation, fill — described in the present tense. Descriptive reading: no target, no forecast.
zones.errorMessage: The zones are not available for this combination.
zones.emptyNone: No zone detected on this combination.

effectiveness.obSpentTitle: Order Block invalidated: the price closed through the zone.
effectiveness.obHeldTitle: Order Block not invalidated: the price did not close through it (the zone holds, even if mitigated or tested).
effectiveness.fvgSpentTitle: Fair Value Gap fully filled: the imbalance is closed.
effectiveness.fvgHeldTitle: Fair Value Gap not fully filled: the imbalance is not closed.

age.withBars: formed {bars, plural, one {# candle} other {# candles}} ago ({duration})

overlaps.heading: Overlaps with other timeframes
overlaps.description: This zone overlaps with zones detected on other timeframes (matching price levels that overlap) — a marker of convergence, not a signal.

actions.analyzeAria: Analyze this zone on the chart
